name: Winter Vegetable Stew/Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 cups slightly aged chopped white mushrooms
1 cup white mushrooms, slightly aged, halved
1 medium yellow onion, cut into large pieces
1 medium carrot, peeled, sliced into half-inch pieces
2 medium russet potatoes, halved then thinly sliced
1 medium fennel bulb, quartered, sliced into half-inch pieces
2 tablespoons fresh thyme, chopped or 2 teaspoons dried thyme
2 tablespoons fresh marjoram, chopped or 2 teaspoons dried marjoram
2 tablespoons fresh parsley, chopped or 2 teaspoons dried parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. Gently simmer the mushroom stock until reduced to about 2 cups liquid.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet; add mushrooms with a pinch of salt and pepper. Sear over high heat until golden.
3. Add half the garlic and deglaze with half the sherry. When nearly dry, transfer mushrooms to a bowl.
4. Heat remaining oil in a stew pot; sauté onion with a pinch of salt and pepper until transparent.
5. Add remaining garlic, rutabaga, carrot, potatoes, and fennel; sauté for 5 minutes.
6. Add mushrooms, herbs, and reduced stock to the pot. Cover and simmer over medium/medium low heat until vegetables are tender (about 30 minutes).
7. Serve with grain, pasta, or polenta and crusty bread, bread sticks, or pretzels.
The Fields of Greens book calls for pouring the mixture into a baking dish, covering with white pastry, and baking at 375°F for 40 minutes.
